description_long: This research institute consists of two main interconnected buildings, one with a modern, rectilinear form clad in a rust-colored material and the other a larger, light gray block with horizontal window bands. The institute is set within a landscaped urban plaza featuring several trees, green patches, and pedestrian walkways. The main entrance is located at the intersection of the two buildings, opening onto a paved area with benches and gathering spaces. Multiple cars are parked along the adjacent street and in a small lot beside the building, indicating vehicular access. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from concrete and metal, with large glass windows providing natural light. The overall design is functional and contemporary, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on accessibility and open public space.
description: A modern research institute with two interconnected buildings—one rust-colored and one light gray—set in a landscaped urban plaza with trees, walkways, and adjacent parking.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance plaza, rust-colored facade, light gray building, parked cars, and landscaped trees.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curved rust-colored building, green patches, and the open pedestrian area with visible benches and trees.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the rust-colored building and the adjacent parking lot with two cars, minimal landscaping visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the full extent of the rust-colored facade, the side of the gray building, and additional parked cars along the street.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is a wide, ground-level opening at the junction of the rust-colored and gray buildings, accessible via a flat paved plaza. It features large glass doors, no visible steps, and is flanked by landscaped areas and benches, providing easy access for all users.

license_info.creator_display_name: Thomas Huc, Enzo Audion, Milan David
